239589.explanation: Disagree. A lower property tax (ozb) will directly lead to fewer or poorer facilities, which often affects residents harder than the tax itself.

239590.explanation: Disagree. Overbetuwe is already attractive. Money should go to local facilities, not expensive promotional campaigns.

239591.explanation: Disagree. Protecting nature is important but not at the expense of vital agriculture. Tailored solutions per location/area.

239592.explanation: GBO opposes municipal financial support for joint energy projects. Not everyone can participate, yet everyone pays. Other subsidies exist and the municipal budget is needed for core tasks affecting all residents. Participants already benefit from lower energy costs themselves.

239593.explanation: We agree with this because new distribution centers cause extra freight traffic, take up space, and harm the landscape and livability in Overbetuwe. Moreover, they hardly provide jobs for people from Overbetuwe.

239594.explanation: Agree. Housing shortage in Overbetuwe is felt by many residents. We take those concerns seriously. At the same time, the municipality has a legal obligation to house status holders. That is why we focus on more affordable housing for everyone.

239595.explanation: We disagree with this statement because we already spend money on integration. Newcomers receive support with language, work, and participation. We believe the effectiveness of current policy should first be carefully reviewed. Spending more money is not automatically better.

239596.explanation: We agree with this because employers in agriculture and horticulture bear responsibility for their operations. Housing seasonal workers is part of that and prevents extra pressure on the housing market and livability in Overbetuwe.

239597.explanation: We disagree because such a strict requirement is too restrictive. Not every company can immediately operate fully sustainably. In Overbetuwe we choose realistic growth and encourage sustainability, not through exclusion.

239598.explanation: Agree. There are already many solar panels in Overbetuwe. Agricultural land is scarce and valuable. We advise restraint and choose rooftops, business parks, and existing locations first, with attention to landscape and public support.

239599.explanation: We disagree because accessibility is primarily the responsibility of entrepreneurs themselves. We encourage entrepreneurs but do not support this financially.

239600.explanation: We agree and already do this. In Overbetuwe we continue this policy because every child has the right to play sports. Lack of money should never be a reason for a child not to participate.

239601.explanation: We agree: parents should have the choice of one place where education, childcare, and after-school care come together. This offers calm and convenience for families. It is not mandatory: not all schools have to comply.

239602.explanation: Agree. But sports clubs must primarily be responsible for their own financial management. The municipality can support with policy and facilities, and in special cases with financial assistance.

239603.explanation: Agree, visible enforcement increases safety and livability in the villages.

239604.explanation: We disagree because police capacity is already under pressure. Weekly consultation hours in every village are inefficient, costly, and often unnecessary. Deployment should focus on emergency help and enforcement, with flexible contact when truly needed.

239605.explanation: We agree because fatbikes increasingly cause unsafe and antisocial behavior. By controlling more often, traffic safety and the sense of security in Overbetuwe improve, especially for pedestrians and cyclists.

239607.explanation: We agree because affordable housing for everyone is important. In Overbetuwe, 30% social housing is needed to give starters, families, and people with lower incomes opportunities. This is existing and necessary policy.

239608.explanation: We disagree with this. Elst is a village without high-rise buildings of this scale. Six floors or higher do not fit the village character.

239609.explanation: We agree. Living on business parks can help with the housing shortage, but only in suitable locations. It must not hinder companies and must fit safety, quality of living, and a clear vision for the site.

239610.explanation: New construction outside village boundaries harms the landscape and village character. At the same time, housing shortages may require tailored solutions. Priority is to build within existing areas and only expand when truly necessary and carefully integrated.

239612.explanation: We disagree. In Overbetuwe, the income limit is already set at 120% of the minimum wage. Raising it further can lead to poverty traps and reduce work incentives. Therefore, we support the current policy.

239613.explanation: We agree that people receiving social assistance benefits should do socially meaningful work where possible. This helps with participation and personal development.

239614.explanation: Subsidized jobs are not a structural solution. However, we find it important to guide and support the long-term unemployed towards regular employment or participation that matches their abilities where possible.

239615.explanation: Providing informal care requires much time and effort. It cannot be expected that social assistance recipients who provide intensive informal care continue to fully apply for jobs. Tailored solutions and temporary relief from this obligation are needed.

239616.explanation: A pilot project for a basic income in Overbetuwe is costly and has uncertain effects. We prefer targeted support and activation of people who need it.

239618.explanation: Disagree. It might be possible in some places but not everywhere. Livability remains the top priority.

239619.explanation: We agree. Free parking at shopping centers keeps shopping accessible, supports local businesses, and prevents visitors from going to other towns. This keeps the center lively and attractive for Overbetuwe residents.

239620.explanation: We agree and already provide this. Tailored transport between the villages in Overbetuwe is important for the accessibility of the elderly and less mobile residents. This way, everyone remains independent and connected to facilities.

239624.explanation: GBO considers the library important for social interaction and development. However, we do not see why a new library in Elst should now have priority over other urgent tasks and investments.

239625.explanation: We are against a large-scale asylum seekers center (AZC) for 300 asylum seekers. GBO stands for a humane and realistic refugee policy. An AZC of 300 places in Elst is too large-scale and does not fit with capacities and livability. We choose small-scale, dispersed housing with perspective and integration.

239626.explanation: Disagree. Safety for cyclists is important but accessibility remains necessary. Therefore, Elst will continue to be an attractive shopping area.

239627.explanation: We agree. The dikes in Overbetuwe are important connecting roads for residents, agriculture, and emergency services. Accessibility for cars and motorcycles ensures reachability, safety, and good access to our villages.

239628.explanation: We disagree. Couples therapy is a personal responsibility and not a core task of the municipality. The municipal budget should be used for youth care and support where there is real need and no alternatives.

239629.explanation: Agree. Support with necessary dental care for low incomes can prevent health problems in Overbetuwe. However, this should be supplemental, targeted, and temporary so that the municipality does not take over a structural national task.

239630.explanation: We agree. Expansion of farms with many animals can lead to extra nitrogen emissions, traffic congestion, and harming the living environment. In Overbetuwe, we choose balance between agriculture, nature, and livability.

239631.explanation: We disagree. Residential caravan sites are already present in Overbetuwe. We think it’s important to properly manage existing facilities and choose a balance in the housing task that fits available space and capacity.

239633.explanation: Agree with reinstating Valburg Station. A station in Valburg improves accessibility and sustainability. However, we believe this should be funded by the national government; Overbetuwe should not bear the financial burden.

239634.explanation: We agree. Those who pay dog tax can expect that this money flows back to dog facilities. In Overbetuwe, this ensures fair spending, better dog walking areas, and less nuisance in public spaces.

239635.explanation: Introduce a blue zone only if residents and entrepreneurs in Driel and/or Heteren want it; otherwise, do not implement it.

239637.explanation: Dorpsstraat must remain accessible, but with room for safety and social interaction.

239638.explanation: We agree. Overbetuwe already makes a large contribution to the energy transition. New wind turbines cause a lot of resistance from local residents and businesses. We choose balance, support, and realistic decisions.

239639.explanation: We disagree. Overbetuwe is spacious and offers enough places to develop nature without removing parking in residential areas. However, we will continue to monitor green and livable environments where possible.

239640.explanation: Starters deserve real opportunities to own their own home.

239641.explanation: We agree. With fewer care providers, the municipality can more effectively focus on quality and essential care. In Overbetuwe, we want support for people who really need it, with oversight, tailor-made solutions, and affordability.

239642.explanation: We agree. The dike between Heteren and Driel is an important connecting route. Opening it on weekends ensures accessibility, spreads traffic, and prevents detours and shortcut traffic for residents and visitors.

239589.explanation: Property tax (ozb) maintains facilities like swimming pools and village halls. Overbetuwe is already one of the cheapest municipalities in the Netherlands with high quality. The CDA strives to keep it that way. Those who cut property tax must be honest: facilities will close and service will worsen.

239590.explanation: Overbetuwe already has a strong business climate with the lowest property tax for companies in the region. We support entrepreneurs through sound economic policy with an entrepreneurs’ desk that the CDA wants to improve. Promotion campaigns are the responsibility of the sector itself, not the municipality using taxpayers’ money.

239591.explanation: The CDA cherishes our farmers and Overbetuwe’s landscape. Nature and agriculture belong together. Farmers are stewards of our land and deserve space to (re)invent their businesses. We help them make sustainability possible instead of taking land away. With gems like Park Lingezegen, we show cooperation is possible.

239592.explanation: The CDA welcomes citizen initiatives and actively facilitates them, for example via energy cooperatives, solar roof projects, and flexible procedures. Financial support is not always necessary or desirable. It should primarily be made feasible with support and without pressuring the municipal budget.

239593.explanation: Overbetuwe already provides sufficient space for large-scale logistics at Park 15. The CDA consciously chooses expansion of SMEs and family businesses that offer sustainable jobs for our residents. Moreover, these companies contribute much more to the local economy and community life.

239594.explanation: Status holders must be housed, but it is not always fair to prioritize them for social rental homes. The CDA chooses temporary housing solutions and building many affordable homes so scarce rental homes remain available to residents who have been waiting for years.

239595.explanation: The municipality already has tasks around guidance and integration which we perform well. Spending extra money is undesirable without proper national funding or clear mandates. We choose effective support within existing resources and prevent rising local taxes.

239596.explanation: Overbetuwe already has policies where the sector itself provides housing for seasonal workers, via permits that reflect actual needs and with supervision on livability. This prevents extra pressure on the housing market.

239597.explanation: Sustainability is important to the CDA but excluding companies affects people’s jobs and income. There are national rules companies must comply with. The municipality should stimulate and guide within those frameworks, not exclude or raise barriers.

239598.explanation: Agricultural land and open landscape are scarce and valuable. In Overbetuwe, the A15 zone is designated for large-scale solar energy and it is full. Therefore, we choose solar panels on roofs, not on fertile farmland.

239599.explanation: Everyone should be able to participate, but it is not the municipality’s task to finance private buildings. Entrepreneurs are responsible for accessibility within national rules. However, we find it important that the entrepreneurs’ desk supports entrepreneurs in this.

239600.explanation: Sports are indispensable for children. If costs are a barrier, the municipality must help so every child can participate, for those who need it.

239601.explanation: For children, it is nice and safe when education, childcare, and activities come together. This provides peace for families and enables good cooperation. Therefore, the CDA supports child centers where children can develop from a young age.

239602.explanation: Sports clubs are indispensable for our livable and healthy villages and run on volunteers. If they face financial problems due to rising costs, the municipality should temporarily step in to restore clubs to financial health.

239603.explanation: Visible enforcement officers in the neighborhood increase safety and quickly address nuisance. Boa’s are an important addition to the police, which is often already overloaded. More presence in our villages creates calm and confidence.

239604.explanation: Safety begins with visible and approachable police in the villages. A fixed consultation hour with the neighborhood officer makes contact easily accessible and helps identify problems early.

239605.explanation: More controls on fatbikes tackle nuisance and make traffic safer for everyone.

239607.explanation: The housing shortage mainly affects people with a smaller budget and starters. By realizing at least 30% social rental housing in new construction, we ensure enough affordable homes and more flow through the housing market.

239608.explanation: Building higher in Elst yields more homes and reserves space for greenery, with facilities and public transport nearby.

239609.explanation: By smartly combining living and working, we make better use of existing space and can build additional homes without continuously developing new areas.

239610.explanation: We build both within the cores, with additional streets in villages, and on larger expansion locations such as near Heteren, Elst, and Zetten. Always carefully and with room for greenery and livability.

239612.explanation: Poverty requires customized solutions. Not everyone needs the same. The municipality must provide targeted help to people who are struggling, instead of only raising general limits.

239613.explanation: Participation in society is important for self-esteem and future opportunities. Social work helps people stay active and increases the chance of a regular job.

239614.explanation: The goal is sustainable work, not temporary subsidized jobs. The municipality must invest in guidance, training, and cooperation with employers so that people genuinely find employment.

239615.explanation: Informal care is of great value and must be accommodated when it is intensive. At the same time, there must be clear agreements to prevent abuse. This way, we support genuine caregivers and keep people active towards work where possible.

239616.explanation: A basic income is quickly accepted but does not structurally solve problems. Moreover, income policy is not a municipal task. We prefer to focus on guidance toward work and targeted support where needed.

239618.explanation: Many people in Overbetuwe depend on the car. Homes without sufficient parking spaces cause pressure and nuisance in residential streets. Therefore, we opt for realistic parking standards.

239619.explanation: Free parking keeps shopping centers accessible and supports local entrepreneurs. This way, our village centers remain attractive to residents and visitors.

239620.explanation: Not everyone can always use a car, and public transport is not suitable everywhere. Tailored transport connects the villages and keeps facilities accessible for the elderly, youth, and people with reduced mobility.

239624.explanation: The existing library in Elst works well and is subsidized by the municipality. Housing belongs to the organization, not to municipal investments.

239625.explanation: Shelter is a legal task. The CDA sees Elst as a logical location due to facilities and accessibility, but only if fully funded by the national government. Small locations, such as 100 places, are financially the municipality’s responsibility, including costs for safety and care.

239626.explanation: A bicycle street does not automatically make Dorpsstraat safer. Measurements show that most people adhere to the speed limits; it is about the behavior of all road users. We choose smart adjustments to the current layout to make the street safer and keep the center accessible.

239627.explanation: The dikes are important connections in our municipality. Accessibility for residents, agriculture, and emergency services is necessary, with attention to safety and livability.

239628.explanation: Problems in families should be identified and supported early, but full reimbursement of relationship therapy is not always a municipal task. We focus on appropriate youth care and prevention where needed.

239629.explanation: Good oral care prevents greater health problems. For people with low incomes, care must remain accessible so that money is not a barrier.

239630.explanation: In our program, we support space for agricultural entrepreneurs who want to become more sustainable and future-proof their business. The number of animals is not decisive, but whether expansion fits within clear rules for the environment, animal welfare, and living environment.

239631.explanation: The municipality currently has six mobile home pitches under construction, in accordance with the ruling of the European Court of Human Rights. In the medium term, expansion may be necessary, but the focus now is on a proper and careful realization.

239633.explanation: The CDA has been lobbying for this for years. The station fits into our mobility plan and regional plans of the GMR Arnhem and Nijmegen. It provides better accessibility for residents, work, and new homes. Therefore, we continue to fully commit to this.

239634.explanation: The dog tax is legally a general tax, just like the property tax (ozb). If it is only linked to dog facilities, a deficit arises that must be compensated by higher property tax.

239635.explanation: Parking measures must be tailored per core and considered together with entrepreneurs and residents. Not everywhere is a blue zone necessary or desirable.

239637.explanation: Dorpsstraat is an important access road for Elst. Keeping it open for cars ensures good accessibility of shops, facilities, and homes without compromising safety.

239638.explanation: Wind turbines have already been approved along the A15 near Oosterhout for large-scale energy generation. Due to distance standards and livability, further placement of large wind turbines in Overbetuwe appears unfeasible. There is still space for small-scale heritage turbines.

239639.explanation: Greening is important, but not by removing necessary parking spaces in residential areas. This ensures that residents' neighborhoods remain accessible and livable.

239640.explanation: In our housing plans, we aim for affordable building in various price segments. A fixed requirement of €250,000 is not realistic and often leads to subsidies, fewer homes in other segments, or more expensive houses to compensate. The CDA chooses a healthy, affordable mix.

239641.explanation: Different care needs require different providers. The CDA chooses quality, collaboration, and cost control, not artificially limiting the number of care providers.

239642.explanation: The dike is important for accessibility of villages and for recreation. The CDA wants space for all road users, with attention to safety, instead of full closure on weekends.

239589.explanation: Increasing or decreasing taxes is not a goal in itself. The proceeds from the property tax must be invested by the municipality in good roads, housing construction, livelihood security, and more greenery in the villages. Taxes must be balanced with the tasks the municipality performs.

239590.explanation: For a municipality where it is pleasant to live, sufficient jobs are important. For GroenLinks-PvdA Overbetuwe, this is a task for government and businesses together. The municipality has already taken up this task in recent years in cooperation with our companies and organizations.

239591.explanation: Together with farmers, we want to work on nature-inclusive agriculture. Part of the farmland can then become nature. There will still be enough farmland left to produce our food.

239592.explanation: We approach the transition and an energy-neutral municipality together. We help residents not only with money but also with knowledge and other actions, such as collective purchasing.

239593.explanation: Distribution centers do not provide local jobs or a sustainable economy. They harm our landscape and our residents receive nothing in return.

239594.explanation: This concerns people with a residence permit, so Dutch nationals. Without a roof over your head, you cannot participate well in society and therefore cannot integrate well. The municipality must also consider other groups who need priority to continue participating.

239595.explanation: Integration of newcomers is done together. The municipality helps, including financially. Without the involvement of Overbetuwe residents, businesses, and organizations, integration will not go well. It is important that the available money is well used.

239596.explanation: Seasonal workers deserve a clean and safe place to live, without losing their home if they lose their job. This often goes wrong in the Netherlands. The employer is primarily responsible here. The municipality must have clear rules for this housing and enforce them.

239597.explanation: Sustainable working is very important for a livable future for our planet and residents. That is why the municipality helps companies and entrepreneurs to work more sustainably. We must support companies that are working towards sustainable practices and keep them in our municipality, not send them away.

239598.explanation: We have already done a lot for sustainable energy generation. As a result, we have sufficient solar energy in Overbetuwe. For the energy challenge, we are looking at other sustainable energy sources. Solar panels on agricultural land require a lot of space and come at the expense of food production and space for nature.

239599.explanation: Accessibility is important for GL-PvdA Overbetuwe. Everyone should be able to participate in society, including people with disabilities. That is why we want to help entrepreneurs with knowledge to make their buildings more accessible. Any costs involved are for the entrepreneur; that is part of doing business.

239600.explanation: Sports are very important for the health and development of children. We are happy with the Overbetuwe Participation Scheme, so that children from poorer families can also participate in sports. This investment gives children equal opportunities and provides benefits such as less loneliness and lower healthcare costs.

239601.explanation: The involved organizations find it easier to communicate if activities are held in one place. This way, children get more opportunities to develop well. If everything cannot be in one place, we will ensure the municipality is safe, so children can go to their destinations comfortably and safely.

239602.explanation: Sports clubs are very important for the health and social cohesion in our municipality. If unexpected situations arise causing a club to get into trouble, the municipality provides support. This can be with knowledge, experience, materials, or possibly a loan.

239603.explanation: Overbetuwe scores well in terms of safety. Municipal enforcement officers can handle the problems. However, there is too little regular police force to do their work properly. We therefore ask the Ministry of Security and Justice to pay attention to this issue.

239604.explanation: The municipality determines together with the police where they should focus most. If the police consider it important to hold a weekly consultation hour, they decide that themselves. Our residents benefit more if neighborhood officers are well accessible at different times and in various ways.

239605.explanation: This is not a big problem in Overbetuwe. If we want to improve traffic safety, there are other actions that have more effect, such as controls on speed, alcohol use, and rude behavior. A good road design also helps to increase traffic safety.

239607.explanation: There are too few homes for young people, starters, and seniors. GL-PvdA therefore wants 30% of the homes in Overbetuwe to be social rental housing, as stated in the municipal housing policy. That is why we need to build more social rental homes now, so that eventually 30% of all homes are social rental.

239608.explanation: The housing crisis means we must manage the limited space in the Netherlands well. By building higher, we create homes for people looking for housing, even in places with little space. We provide enough greenery in the neighborhood and create space for people to meet each other.

239609.explanation: Living is not just about walls and a roof, but also about a healthy, green, and livable neighborhood. This combination of functions makes it difficult to live comfortably. Our entrepreneurs must be able to continue working well at their business locations.

239610.explanation: For a future-proof agricultural sector and a livable rural area, small-scale housing or the construction of tiny houses is important. Adding greenery is a requirement. This benefits both farmers and other residents of the countryside.

239612.explanation: People living in poverty are more likely to have health problems and often feel lonely. In a wealthy country like the Netherlands, no one should fall below a certain income. That is why we raise the income limit, so that everyone in Overbetuwe can participate and we fight inequality.

239613.explanation: Social assistance is part of the social safety net. Even more obligations and rules only make things harder for people. We guide people who can work to meaningful jobs for a fair wage.

239614.explanation: People who participate in society are healthier and happier. Work helps with that. We want to help people who have been out of work for a long time by giving them work that is useful for themselves and society. This is already working well in other municipalities.

239615.explanation: Informal caregivers do incredibly important and heavy work. They contribute usefully to society and therefore should not be forced by the municipality to do even more if they are on social assistance. That causes extra stress and health complaints for both the caregiver and the person cared for.

239616.explanation: In many municipalities, various forms of a basic income have already been tested. A basic income replaces the complicated and expensive benefits system. People can make their own choices without the government doubting whether they are entitled to money. This way, no one falls below a certain income.

239618.explanation: By promoting shared cars and making public space mainly suitable for cyclists and pedestrians, people become less dependent on a car. This creates more space for housing. For people who need a car due to their health, accessibility must always be ensured.

239619.explanation: In our villages, parking is free. There remains space for cars, but a parking spot might be a little further to walk. For people with disabilities, there are enough disabled parking spaces available. The design of public spaces encourages people to walk or cycle.

239620.explanation: National budget cuts have made public transport expensive and poor. Due to the disappearance of bus lines, villages are less accessible and less pleasant to live in. Everyone has a right to affordable and good transport. That is why we invest in transport between our villages.

239624.explanation: Reading is fun and important for all ages. The library is also a meeting place. GroenLinks-PvdA Overbetuwe is proud of the library in Elst and we are very happy with the extended opening hours. In our opinion, a new library in Elst adds nothing.

239625.explanation: Overbetuwe has always been a hospitable municipality, for example with shelter for Ukrainians in Zetten and crisis emergency shelter in Elst. By law, Overbetuwe must now accommodate 297 refugees. We provide good shelter that considers the neighborhood and is affordable for the municipality.

239626.explanation: Cars currently have a lot of space in the Dorpsstraat, which leaves too little room for cyclists. This often causes dangerous situations. The municipal mobility plan actually allows plenty of space for cyclists and pedestrians. Designing the Dorpsstraat as a bicycle street is a good step.

239627.explanation: Dikes are often dangerous places for cyclists and cars because other road users drive too fast. For safety, it is sometimes necessary to prohibit cars and motorcycles from driving on the dike. This way cyclists can safely travel from one village to another.

239628.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA Overbetuwe finds it strange that a distinction is made between couples with and without children. Municipalities receive less money from the government. As a result, we must make difficult choices. This means we will not pay for relationship therapy.

239629.explanation: Dental costs are difficult to insure. A poor set of teeth can make it hard to participate fully in society. For people with little money, this is a problem. Therefore, we contribute to dental care if needed, but not to a dental insurance.

239630.explanation: Our soil is best suited for arable farming and fruit cultivation. That is why we want to support these sectors. We want to strengthen agriculture, for example by taking more account of nature. Intensive livestock farming does not fit with our plans for Overbetuwe.

239631.explanation: Traveller residents have the right to a home that suits them, just like everyone else. In Overbetuwe, we have recently finally been building new traveller sites faster, but there are still not nearly enough. That is why we continue to work on providing more places.

239633.explanation: Everyone deserves safe, sustainable, and affordable transport. In recent years, the government in The Hague has cut a lot in this area. GroenLinks-PvdA Overbetuwe wants to invest in affordable and accessible public transport. That is why we advocate for the return of Valburg Station.

239634.explanation: This is a type of tax whose money could also be used for other important things. GroenLinks-PvdA Overbetuwe wants fewer types of taxes. If the total tax revenue remains the same, the dog tax can be eliminated. In many municipalities, this already works well.

239635.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA Overbetuwe finds this completely unnecessary. Moreover, it costs time and money to introduce and enforce this. Residents and visitors of Driel and Heteren are more inconvenienced by this than it provides benefits. The same applies to our enforcement officers.

239637.explanation: Cars currently have a lot of space in the Dorpsstraat, which leaves too little room for cyclists. This often causes dangerous situations. The municipal mobility plan actually allows plenty of space for cyclists and pedestrians. Designing the Dorpsstraat as a bicycle street is a good step.

239638.explanation: Overbetuwe already does a lot in generating sustainable energy. We do not want more large solar parks that take up a lot of space and damage nature. That is why we are also looking at wind turbines. We take into account the people living there and the opportunities for future housing construction.

239639.explanation: More greenery in the neighborhood creates a healthier environment for humans and animals and helps against heat stress. The municipality has recently worked hard on more greenery, for example by participating in removing pavement tiles and giving residents free trees and plants. We want to continue this.

239640.explanation: The housing crisis requires that we carefully consider which homes are most needed. Homes are unaffordable for many people. Therefore, GroenLinks-PvdA Overbetuwe also wants to focus on building affordable homes. These are smaller but of good quality.

239641.explanation: Working with fewer care providers makes it easier to make agreements about the care needed. We prefer to work with care providers who have permanent employees. This way we can provide good care. Residents who need (specialist) care must be able to receive it.

239642.explanation: Dikes are often dangerous places for cyclists and cars because other road users drive too fast. For safety, it is sometimes necessary to prohibit cars and motorcycles from driving on the dike. This way cyclists can safely travel from one village to another.

239589.explanation: Overbetuwe has a very low property tax compared to other municipalities. Lowering the property tax means cutting back on care, welfare, maintenance of greenery and roads, or other facilities. For someone with a house worth 500,000 euros, it concerns only a few tens of euros per year to keep this up.

239590.explanation: Overbetuwe should focus on innovative, sustainable entrepreneurs in energy, care, and high tech. We especially focus on self-employed persons and small and medium enterprises. They are the job creators and have more ties to the region than multinationals.

239591.explanation: By converting part of the agricultural land into nature, (nitrogen) space is created to be able to build. Farmers must of course be adequately compensated and receive perspective for a different use of their company, possibly in another location.

239592.explanation: Locally generating and using energy helps against grid congestion. The municipality can help start energy cooperatives of residents. This does not always have to be financial help, but sometimes it helps to realize an initiative.

239593.explanation: Overbetuwe already has enough distribution warehouses. D66 prefers to provide space for SMEs that contribute more to regional employment and the economy.

239594.explanation: This is national legislation that Overbetuwe simply has to comply with. It concerns a maximum of 10% social housing. The long waiting lists and people sometimes not being able to find any housing at all are due to too little social housing being built. Building more is the solution.

239595.explanation: The faster newcomers integrate, the sooner they can make a valuable contribution to society. If a newcomer can work instead of relying on benefits or other support, you actually save money. It is also much better for the newcomers themselves.

239596.explanation: Seasonal workers need temporary housing and therefore often have different housing needs than regular home seekers. By working together with employers, alternative solutions can be found, so they do not have to rely on ordinary homes, which are already in short supply.

239597.explanation: Ultimately, all companies must use raw materials and energy sustainably. It’s better to require this immediately when a new company comes to our municipality. If you want to make agreements about this after the company is already there, it becomes much more difficult and expensive.

239598.explanation: Placing solar panels on fertile agricultural land is a waste. It is not only bad for agriculture but also for biodiversity. We already meet our target for solar parks up to 2030. After that, other options must be considered to be climate neutral by 2050.

239599.explanation: Accessibility belongs to an inclusive society. We do that together. The municipality provides public buildings, entrepreneurs for their own premises. For small(er) entrepreneurs adjustments are probably difficult to realize, and then the municipality can support them.

239600.explanation: Sports and culture are important for the social development of children and their health. Children of parents or guardians with little money deserve the same chances to develop their talents and live healthily. Therefore, this must be an integral part of poverty policy.

239601.explanation: D66 supports broad schools and integrated child centers where education, care and after-school care collaborate in the interest of the child.

239602.explanation: The municipality already contributes via sports fields, sports halls and their maintenance. If there are (financial) problems, we will look together at the causes and possible solutions. More money is not always the solution. Sometimes more creativity and cooperation are needed.

239603.explanation: With the current staffing of the community enforcement officers (BOAs), it is difficult to be present throughout the municipality. But BOAs are not a replacement for the police. For more safety, more police officers are especially needed, and that should be addressed nationally.

239604.explanation: D66 finds it important that the neighborhood police officer is easily accessible and frequently visible in the area. A consultation hour can be useful if people actually come by. The police should look together with village representatives at what is appropriate.

239605.explanation: Antisocial behavior is wrong in any case and leads to a feeling of insecurity among others. It is not only about fatbikes. It is primarily a reason to further explore why someone behaves antisocially.

239607.explanation: There is a shortage of social rental homes and therefore the waiting time is long. Besides that 30%, the type of housing is important. For example, affordable senior housing (senior courtyards) must be added so that the flow improves.

239608.explanation: D66 considers buildings of 5 to 8 floors acceptable in new residential areas in Elst. This prevents further building in rural areas. In older neighborhoods, high-rise is not always appropriate with existing buildings.

239609.explanation: This does not solve the housing shortage. Homes on industrial sites can lead to problems with nuisance zones, which means companies cannot grow or cannot settle there at all. That does not fit our aim to strengthen the business climate.

239610.explanation: If the existing built-up area is meant here, that is not realistic given we have to build thousands of houses. D66 wants to use our rural area sparingly, but sometimes it is good if, for example, a barn is demolished and houses are built in its place.

239612.explanation: People who really need it must be generously helped without bureaucracy. But even better is to offer people opportunities to develop so they are not dependent on poverty policy. Raising the income limit does not help with that and increases the poverty trap.

239613.explanation: Most people want to make a meaningful contribution to society. That helps build a social environment and a sense of self-worth. Socially meaningful work should focus on participation, reintegration and increasing chances for suitable work.

239614.explanation: Those jobs already exist and help guide the long-term unemployed step by step to suitable work. That is better for the unemployed and cheaper for the municipality because the number of benefits decreases.

239615.explanation: That does not always have to be a full-time job. As far as D66 is concerned, a good balance should be sought between work and informal care.

239616.explanation: D66 supports the idea of a basic income but does not think Overbetuwe should start a pilot now. Many adjustments are needed in existing rules, which costs a lot of time and money. It is better to learn from other municipalities (like Wageningen) where pilots are currently underway.

239618.explanation: The developer saves on construction costs and residents park in public places, which can cause nuisance for the neighborhood. Reducing the parking standard is sensible in neighborhoods where there are good alternatives to the car, such as good public transport and bike paths.

239619.explanation: Free parking helps keep our centers lively and is good for our local entrepreneurs.

239620.explanation: In some centers, you can barely get around in the evenings or weekends with regular public transport. Then tailored transport helps people get somewhere. That can also be a van driven by volunteers. That is cheaper and certainly just as pleasant.

239624.explanation: D66 supports a modern library in a multifunctional center for culture and socializing at an accessible and appealing location with extended opening hours. The library is much more than lending books. It is a place for meeting and exchanging knowledge for young and old.

239625.explanation: D66 advocates humane reception of refugees. These 300 is a reasonable number considering the distribution across the Netherlands. Then reception and safety are paid for by the COA. At a smaller location, we have to arrange and pay much more ourselves, which comes at the expense of other things.

239626.explanation: With a bike street or separate lane for cyclists and cars, shops remain accessible. It is safer for cars, cyclists and shoppers. And more space for greenery will be created. The condition is that sufficient, free and easily accessible parking spaces are available at the edge of the village.

239627.explanation: Overbetuwe has quite a few dike roads used daily by many people commuting. For D66, traffic safety is the most important criterion. Unless that is at risk, they should just remain open.

239628.explanation: Here people have their own responsibility. The municipality is not intended and not able to solve people's relationship problems. The municipality does provide youth care if needed for children, so they don't suffer from the home situation.

239629.explanation: Basic dental care (at least an annual check-up) should be part of healthcare costs reimbursed through poverty policy. If people avoid care because of cost, that only leads to higher costs and poorer health in the long term.

239630.explanation: Further growth in the number of animals does not fit in a time when developments (like housing construction) are stalled and is also not good for animal welfare.

239631.explanation: We are still behind on our legal duty and the agreements made.

239633.explanation: According to D66, that is not realistic. It will cost a lot of money while it is unclear what it will yield. Valburg has other priorities where we should focus our energy, such as housing for the elderly and a green buffer against the existing infrastructure.

239634.explanation: It is illogical that dog owners would pay more than what they get in return. If all costs, both for construction and maintenance and costs for enough DepoDogs, are calculated, the municipality does not make a profit from dog tax.

239635.explanation: The parking spaces at shopping centers (like Polenplein in Driel) are meant for shoppers for whom walking or cycling is not a good option. For free long-term parking, other parking spaces a little further away should be available.

239637.explanation: The current situation is unsafe. It is not logical at all to drive through the entire Dorpstraat if you have to be on the other side. D66 wants a future vision on shopping, living, accessibility, parking, greenery and facilities to keep Elst’s center attractive.

239638.explanation: We have agreed on 2 search areas where wind turbines may be placed if they comply with the standards (including effects on residents). Wind is a sustainable energy source needed for energy supply now and in the future.

239639.explanation: More greenery in the neighborhood is good against heat stress and drought. Besides, it makes the neighborhood much cozier than all that stone. In neighborhoods where there are already too few spots, you should not remove them. But a few extra trees or a strip of grass instead of a parking spot is of course not nature.

239640.explanation: Such a requirement makes new construction projects unaffordable and then they won't get off the ground at all.

239641.explanation: By working with a limited number of contracted providers, better agreements can be made regarding the quality of care and support. However, there must be a sufficient supply so that people can choose which care provider they want.

239642.explanation: The Cora Baltussenallee and the Boltweg are sufficient on weekends for cars and motorcycles traveling from Heteren to Driel, or vice versa. Then the Drielse Rijndijk can be closed off, except for local traffic. This makes it safer and more attractive for cyclists and other tourists.

239589.explanation: The municipality must first put its internal financial affairs in order. The property tax for 2026 has been raised unnecessarily.

239590.explanation: There is already a shortage of business parks. Attracting additional companies will cause problems there.

239592.explanation: The municipality should provide support but not financial assistance.

239593.explanation: Establishment depends on location, scale, and employment. We are critical and look at the benefits and burdens for Overbetuwe, its entrepreneurs, and residents. If, for example, XL establishments do not provide employment for the local market, then we are opposed.

239594.explanation: The municipality must simply comply with the national target for housing status holders.

239595.explanation: Existing organizations must collaborate better on this dossier. That is the solution.

239596.explanation: We believe it is desirable that employers bear both the benefits and burdens of seasonal workers. It is therefore logical that employers also bear the costs of the additional housing needs. This also applies to labor migrants working in logistics.

239597.explanation: Companies have always been required to comply with national sustainability standards.

239599.explanation: Entrepreneurs themselves are responsible. The municipality must enforce the standards.

239601.explanation: For parents, children, and professionals, combining education, childcare, and extracurricular activities in one location offers many advantages. Where there is no space for this in existing situations, we look for other solutions.

239602.explanation: The municipality must create good conditions for associations. Charge reasonable fees for facilities (fields, accommodations), make membership easily accessible for youth, target groups, and people with low incomes, and promote daytime use of accommodations. Do not unnecessarily burden volunteers with all kinds of rules.

239603.explanation: We are thinking here of hiring enforcement officers (BOAs) who are deployed at specific locations at specific times to enforce regulations.

239604.explanation: There are more effective and efficient ways to ensure good contact between police, residents, and entrepreneurs. Neighborhood and village councils can also play a valuable role here.

239605.explanation: The municipality has limited influence on police priorities. We primarily focus on good contact between police, residents, and entrepreneurs so that they recognize these priorities. More concretely, our priorities are tackling undermining crime, increasing resilience against digital threats, traffic safety, and combating burglary, robberies, street theft, and violent crimes.

239607.explanation: This is already the principle and there is a legislative proposal to make it mandatory nationwide.

239608.explanation: This does not fit with the rural character of the village.

239609.explanation: Except for housing for labor migrants.

239610.explanation: However, the municipality must pay attention to preserving the rural character of the surroundings.

239612.explanation: We want to better reach people below the current income threshold. Especially among self-employed and recent retirees, there is a relatively large group that does not claim benefits, although they are entitled to them. Good cooperation with housing corporations, Food Bank volunteers, Stichting Leergeld, Noodfonds Overbetuwe, and Forte Welzijn is important here.

239613.explanation: Doing socially meaningful work is often good for both community and individual. But it should be encouraged rather than made compulsory.

239614.explanation: Guiding people who need it toward employment ranges from conversations with counselors to using tools such as job coaching, wage cost subsidies, and the no-risk policy for illness or disability. Our municipality’s current efforts are described in the reintegration implementation plan 2025. In that plan, we mainly focus on residents who do not sufficiently master the language (status holders), young people, residents registered in the target group register, and residents who can quickly be guided to work.

239615.explanation: People with paid jobs usually have to combine informal care and paid work in some way. Just as an employer discusses this with an employee with caregiving tasks, a counselor should do the same for someone receiving social assistance. However, automatically exempting them from the job application requirement is undesirable.

239616.explanation: This is not a priority for us because it also requires considerable personnel (civil service) effort.

239618.explanation: We believe the municipality should use a realistic parking standard. Not based on how we hope people will travel tomorrow. After all, if that hope does not materialize, residents will have a problem. Before you know it, you end up with regulation, permit parking, or paid parking. We do not think that fits with Overbetuwe.

239619.explanation: This discussion mainly takes place in Elst. For a vibrant center, accessibility by car is important.

239620.explanation: Public transport is the responsibility of the province.

239624.explanation: In our view, only a new library is not the solution. Attention must be given to eliminating the fragmented supply of locations for low-threshold welfare functions.

239625.explanation: We support an asylum seekers center (azc) with a maximum of 100 asylum seekers.

239626.explanation: We appreciate the accessibility of the Dorpsstraat for all types of traffic. Regardless of the form, as long as safety is guaranteed.

239627.explanation: We consider good accessibility for residents important, which is why we support keeping it open.

239628.explanation: We disagree with this general statement. If couples therapy is not reimbursed by health insurance, that is not a reason for the municipality to reimburse it. Moreover, there is a regional scheme that addresses such situations, the digital divorce counter.

239629.explanation: The number of applications for special assistance at the municipality or for help with dental costs at the Noodfonds Overbetuwe has been low for years, as the municipality answered questions from us and the Christian Union. We do monitor the effects of the abolition of the municipal insurance policy as of 2026, which included dental insurance. From 2026 onwards, municipal funds will be used, among other things, to compensate for the mandatory deductible on health insurance.

239630.explanation: Within national and provincial frameworks, the municipality of Overbetuwe has set rules via the rural area zoning plan (2013) and the umbrella plan Plus Policy (2020). Expansion of non-ground-based livestock farms is possible after good dialogue with the surroundings, with landscape measures and applying measures related to environment, animal welfare, and sustainable energy. We agreed to this policy at the time.

239631.explanation: Municipalities are obligated to provide sufficient caravan sites. Based on a needs assessment, it was concluded that additional sites are required. Work is now underway on this, together with housing corporations and involving local residents. An allocation policy has also been established.

239633.explanation: A train connection does not seem realistic at this moment.

239634.explanation: We find the idea of basing the amount of the dog tax in the long term on the expenses made for dog facilities a sympathetic one. However, at this moment, this is only the case for slightly more than half. Agreeing with this statement would immediately create a budget gap of 200,000 euros. We do not yet have coverage for that.

239635.explanation: In our opinion, there is no problem that calls for such a solution. Also, the necessary capacity to enforce (to prevent people from moving their parking permit after 2 hours) is not available.

239637.explanation: Many visitors and entrepreneurs appreciate the accessibility of the center of Elst. That must remain so. Also for visitors from the Arnhem and Nijmegen regions.

239638.explanation: Except for the 2 current search areas along the A15.

239639.explanation: We believe that the municipality should base parking standards on realistic figures, based on CROW parking standards and car ownership. Not based on how we hope people will travel tomorrow. After all, if that hope doesn’t come true, residents will have a problem. Before you know it, you end up with regulation through permit parking or paid parking. We do not think that fits with Overbetuwe.

239640.explanation: Given the current market, this is not realistic.

239641.explanation: The municipality must contract care providers based on quality and price, to fulfill its care obligation and taking into account the desirability of some choice freedom for youth and their parents. At the same time, the number of contracted providers must make it possible to maintain overview and control, and to select the right and affordable help per situation. So it's about balance.

239642.explanation: We find good accessibility for the residents of Heteren and Driel important; therefore, we support opening.

239589.explanation: Taxes for residents and entrepreneurs, like the property tax (ozb), should not rise or rise very little. Because the property tax sharply increases in 2026, it must go down again afterward.

239590.explanation: Companies provide jobs, facilities, and pay taxes. Therefore, the municipality must ensure a good business climate and attract companies that fit with Overbetuwe. A promotional campaign is not necessary for this.

239591.explanation: Overbetuwe is a rural municipality. Agriculture is important for our economy and the landscape. We must be careful with farms. Nature and agriculture can go well together, as can be seen in Park Lingezegen.

239592.explanation: The municipality has little money and there are already many subsidies. The municipality can help residents and initiatives with knowledge and advice but cannot give extra money.

239593.explanation: There are already many large distribution centers in Overbetuwe. We want a good mix of different types of companies. Moreover, distribution centers add extra pressure on the housing market due to labor migrants.

239594.explanation: There is a great shortage of housing. The municipality is legally obliged to provide housing for status holders, but they should not receive priority over residents who are also urgently looking for a home.

239595.explanation: Integration is important, but spending extra money is not the solution. It mainly needs to be organized more efficiently.

239596.explanation: Seasonal workers must have decent housing. The employer must arrange this themselves—without causing nuisance to the surroundings.

239597.explanation: What is sustainable and who decides that? Companies are indispensable; they provide employment, facilities, and pay taxes. Of course, they must comply with legal standards.

239598.explanation: There are already large solar parks in Overbetuwe. We want to prevent even more agricultural land from disappearing.

239599.explanation: The accessibility of buildings is important. It is the responsibility of entrepreneurs to make their business properly accessible. The municipality should not spend money on this.

239600.explanation: There are already arrangements, and many clubs already accept children of parents with little money. Allocating extra money is not necessary.

239601.explanation: Children develop better in a familiar environment and need to travel less. That is safer as well.

239602.explanation: Sports and other clubs are important for health and social cohesion. If they get into trouble, the municipality can temporarily help.

239603.explanation: It is a core task of the municipality to ensure safety. If there is less police, the municipality must deploy more enforcement officers (boa's).

239604.explanation: Police and enforcement officers should focus on situations and locations that residents experience as unsafe. A consultation hour can help with this.

239605.explanation: Fatbikes cause a feeling of insecurity among many people and deserve extra attention from enforcement and police.

239607.explanation: Many people are looking for affordable homes. New construction must include a share of affordable housing, such as social rental and affordable/cheap owner-occupied homes.

239608.explanation: Building upwards is necessary to provide enough new homes. That is not possible everywhere, but in some places, it can fit the character of the environment.

239609.explanation: Living in business parks causes nuisance for residents and limitations for companies. That is not desirable.

239610.explanation: If we only build within the existing villages, there will not be enough new housing. Expansion is needed and possible in some places.

239612.explanation: Overbetuwe already has good arrangements for people with little money. If the income limit gets too high, working no longer pays off. Moreover, the municipality has no money for further expansion.

239613.explanation: People who cannot work receive support. We may ask those people to contribute in another way if they can.

239614.explanation: People who have not found work for a long time can be helped with a subsidized job to gain experience. Then moving on to a 'paid' job becomes easier.

239615.explanation: Informal caregiving is important, but exceptions to the job application obligation cause a lot of uncertainty, extra rules, and controls.

239616.explanation: The municipality does not have enough money, knowledge, and capacity for a basic income trial. Other tasks have priority.

239618.explanation: Many residents in our municipality depend on the car. Fewer parking spaces do not lead to more public transport use but do cause parking problems.

239619.explanation: Shops in our village centers are extremely important for livability. Free parking attracts visitors. Paid parking causes fewer customers and shop vacancy.

239620.explanation: The province organizes public transport. The municipality can assist initiatives such as a dial-a-ride bus if financial support is needed.

239624.explanation: Already a lot of money goes to libraries annually. They must ensure a good library with that money.

239625.explanation: Overbetuwe must house about 300 asylum seekers by law. That is proportionally just as much as other municipalities. If we house fewer people, the national government will not fully reimburse the costs, and residents have to pay along. We do not want that.

239626.explanation: The Dorpsstraat was recently adjusted, which cost a lot of money. The municipality has little money now. Improvements for traffic safety are good, but a new and expensive redesign of the Dorpsstraat is not needed, in our opinion.

239627.explanation: The road is for everyone, including cars and motorcycles. If speeding on dikes causes nuisance or unsafe situations, the police should strictly enforce the rules and issue fines.

239628.explanation: A good relationship between parents is important for children. However, we do not believe that the municipality should pay for relationship therapy. The municipality has limited funds and must make choices.

239629.explanation: People can insure themselves against dental costs. Special arrangements already exist for low-income individuals. Since these arrangements are already in place, the municipality does not need to contribute extra to dental costs.

239630.explanation: Farming businesses are important for our rural municipality. Large agricultural companies must also comply with strict environmental rules. If they do so, their expansion should not be automatically blocked.

239631.explanation: Additional mobile home sites are already being created in Overbetuwe. Such a site costs the municipality more than a social rental home. Because many people urgently need housing, the available funds must be used to help as many people as possible.

239633.explanation: A new station in Valburg is worth trying. It could improve public transport connections between Zetten, Valburg, and Elst.

239634.explanation: Dog tax must have a clear purpose. The money should be used for dog walking areas and for keeping public spaces clean.

239635.explanation: Parking is important for shops, and shops are important for a livable village. If there are too few parking spaces at certain times or places, parking with a parking disc can be a good solution. This should be done in consultation with residents and shopkeepers.

239637.explanation: For successful shops in Elst, it is important that people can come by car and park for free. More greenery and extra attention to traffic safety could make Dorpsstraat more attractive.

239638.explanation: The municipality has already approved six wind turbines at Park 15 and towards Dodewaard. This allows us to meet our goals. More wind turbines cause nuisance and make housing development more difficult. Therefore, additional wind turbines are undesirable.

239639.explanation: Green spaces in the neighborhood provide a pleasant living environment and are good for health and water retention. Parking spaces may only be removed if there are too many, which is almost never the case.

239640.explanation: There is a large housing shortage in Overbetuwe. It is important that people who do not qualify for social rental housing can also find a home. Therefore, part of the new construction should consist of cheap and affordable owner-occupied homes.

239641.explanation: The municipality works with many care providers. Often they do good work, but there are currently too many. This makes it harder to monitor quality, collaborate well, and it causes a lot of administration and costs.

239642.explanation: The road is for everyone, including cars and motorcycles. If speeding on dikes causes nuisance or unsafe situations, the police should strictly enforce the rules and issue fines.

239589.explanation: Property tax (ozb) is a good way to distribute costs as fairly as possible. To maintain facilities, it is no longer sustainable for the municipality to have one of the lowest ozb rates in the province of Gelderland and the Netherlands.

239590.explanation: To prevent vacancy and possibly deterioration in the long term.

239591.explanation: Nature is important for the wellbeing of people and animals. Converting agricultural land into nature is a good solution when farmers stop farming.

239592.explanation: Instead of financial support, guidance can be requested.

239593.explanation: Large-scale distribution centers spoil the open landscape. They also increase the number of migrant workers who need good housing, which is scarce.

239594.explanation: In addition to the legal obligation to house asylum seekers, we also have a moral responsibility to accommodate displaced fellow humans.

239595.explanation: Integration of newcomers is important. Before considering more funding, the involvement of social organizations, schools, churches, and businesses should be strengthened.

239596.explanation: And that also means qualitatively good housing.

239597.explanation: We have a shared responsibility to care for the world, and sustainable practices as a standard contribute to that.

239598.explanation: Agricultural land is scarce and needed for (nature-inclusive) agriculture. Solar panels can be placed on roofs or facades.

239599.explanation: Entrepreneurs are themselves responsible for making buildings accessible to everyone. Instead of financial support, advice can be requested from the municipality.

239600.explanation: We already do this through the Participation Scheme, which must be retained.

239601.explanation: That can be good for children's development, depending on the situation. However, it is not always practically or financially feasible.

239602.explanation: Associations are important for a thriving municipality. Equal treatment of all types of associations in case of financial difficulties is important, not just sports clubs. Needs may vary. Financial support is a last and temporary remedy in exceptional cases with solid justification.

239603.explanation: Safety is important. More enforcement does not automatically mean more safety. Residents can also be encouraged to contribute to more safety by being vigilant in their own neighborhood.

239604.explanation: The community officer must be accessible and reachable for residents. More clarity about how and when the community officer can be approached helps more than spending the officer’s valuable time on a consultation hour.

239605.explanation: Police controls must primarily focus on safety. And that is not just about fatbikes.

239607.explanation: The current agreement to build at least 30% rental homes must remain.

239608.explanation: With careful integration, 6 or 7 floors in Elst are quite feasible.

239609.explanation: Living in industrial areas is often not good for residents’ well-being and can limit companies’ opportunities. Exceptions for temporary housing of migrant workers can be considered if well justified.

239610.explanation: On the edges of the villages, building is allowed by adding a 'little street' principle. Vacant agricultural buildings may be replaced by homes to keep the countryside lively. But this must be done moderately and carefully so as not to obstruct farmers.

239612.explanation: Raising the income limit creates the risk that working no longer pays off. However, the income limit may grow with inflation.

239613.explanation: Engaging in meaningful activities, if people are able, is important for their wellbeing and also for society.

239614.explanation: There are facilities for unemployed people to find meaningful ways to spend their time. In cases of long-term unemployment, it is important to first identify causes and continue seeking possibilities.

239615.explanation: Informal care is becoming increasingly important. Sometimes it cannot be avoided and often demands a lot from everyone involved. Care for people, professional or voluntary, is meaningful. With clear conditions, it can be decided that applying for jobs during informal caregiving is not appropriate.

239616.explanation: The municipality of Overbetuwe faces many challenges and is not sufficiently equipped for a basic income trial.

239618.explanation: Public transport is not optimal in many villages, and the car remains important. Fewer parking spaces than before is a good step forward.

239619.explanation: Free parking at shopping centers makes them easily accessible and attractive. It reinforces the village character that we want to preserve in our municipality.

239620.explanation: Public transport is a provincial responsibility. The municipality must urge the province to pay attention to the importance of good public transport between our villages. The municipality can facilitate customized transport initiated by residents (e.g., community bus for Heteren, Driel, Elst).

239624.explanation: The library must remain accessible and safe, but that does not automatically mean that money must be invested in a new building.

239625.explanation: We have a legal obligation to accommodate 297 people. Out of care for each other, we want to contribute to the sheltering of displaced and often vulnerable people.

239626.explanation: It would be nice to designate the Dorpsstraat as a bicycle and pedestrian street. However, for smooth car traffic flow, good accessibility to shops and hospitality, and to prevent parking bottlenecks elsewhere, this is not realistic.

239627.explanation: Dikes and floodplains are beautiful. Everyone is allowed to enjoy them. If necessary for safety and preserving the dikes, closing parts of a dike to fast traffic for a few hours a week can be investigated.

239628.explanation: A breakup has major, often negative, consequences for children. Financially helping parents to prevent relationship problems benefits both parents and children and may help avoid higher costs and housing problems in the long term.

239629.explanation: Dental costs are often high, and when facing financial difficulties, people often cut back on dental care. This usually leads to even higher costs later. Therefore, it is good to financially help people not to postpone dental care.

239630.explanation: Intensive livestock farming is not sustainable and poses risks to public health. It is therefore wise to prevent expansions involving large numbers of animals.

239631.explanation: Expansion is underway, but it is progressing far too slowly.

239633.explanation: Residents of Valburg can use the bus service to the stations in Elst and Zetten. There is no need for a station in Valburg.

239634.explanation: In Overbetuwe, we want good facilities for dogs. Residents without dogs should not have to contribute to the costs.

239635.explanation: Facilities in Heteren and Driel are limited and mainly involve shops with short-term parking. Other parking spaces can be used by residents.

239637.explanation: It would be nice to designate the Dorpsstraat as a bicycle and pedestrian street. However, for smooth car traffic flow, good accessibility to shops and hospitality, and to prevent parking bottlenecks elsewhere, this is not realistic.

239638.explanation: To keep life on Earth possible for future generations, we must deploy all sustainable energy sources. Wind turbines are also needed for this, as long as there are no even more sustainable solutions.

239639.explanation: When, according to the new guidelines (Parking Regulation 2025), there are more than enough parking spaces, the municipality must consult with residents to determine which places will be replaced by green areas.

239640.explanation: To promote the transition from rental to owner-occupied homes, there is a need for more affordable owner-occupied housing. This can also include smaller homes or apartments aimed at seniors and starters. Twenty percent would be nice but is probably too ambitious to keep housing construction feasible.

239641.explanation: It is good to have variety in the supply and to keep organizations sharp, also during new tenders.

239642.explanation: Dikes and floodplains are beautiful. Everyone is allowed to enjoy them. If necessary for safety and preserving the dikes, closing parts of a dike to fast traffic for a few hours a week can be investigated.

239589.explanation: The property tax (ozb) is an important source of income for the municipality. If we lower it, we will have to obtain money elsewhere or cut our expenses.

239590.explanation: It is important to be attractive for businesses to establish themselves, but this mainly concerns space, accessibility, and sufficient personnel. Spending money on promotion alone is not enough.

239591.explanation: If agricultural land is designated for another use, the land can also be used for housing construction.

239592.explanation: This is already arranged at the national level, and the municipality does not have a budget for it.

239593.explanation: Distribution centers create employment, and that is important for the economy.

239594.explanation: There are too few affordable homes in Overbetuwe. Giving priority to status holders disadvantages starters from Overbetuwe.

239595.explanation: Integration is important but does not depend on money alone. Integration has many aspects (language, information, education, participation), all of which are important.

239596.explanation: The municipality checks whether the housing meets the municipal requirements.

239597.explanation: It is important that companies operate sustainably. But exclusion goes too far for us.

239598.explanation: The national policy is 'no, unless.' There is no reason for Overbetuwe to be stricter in this.

239599.explanation: Entrepreneurs are legally obliged to make their buildings accessible to people with disabilities. That is part of the normal business operations.

239600.explanation: Participation in sports is important for the development of children and financial barriers should be removed as much as possible.

239601.explanation: If parents choose this, that is fine. As far as we are concerned, it should not be mandatory.

239602.explanation: The cause of the problems must first be investigated and whether they can be resolved in other ways.

239603.explanation: A sense of safety is important for the livability of the communities. Currently, there are too few municipal enforcement officers (boa's) in Overbetuwe to provide that sense of safety to every community.

239604.explanation: It is unclear whether there is a need for that. At present, the current deployment of the community police officer seems to function well.

239605.explanation: Every road user must be addressed regarding antisocial behavior.

239607.explanation: There are too few affordable homes in Overbetuwe. A boost to at least 30% is important.

239608.explanation: Elst is a village with an urban appearance. Residential flats with multiple floors fit within this. However, six floors or higher is too tall for us.

239609.explanation: Industrial sites often have lower requirements for noise disturbance and have different infrastructure. Company housing can, of course, be located on an industrial site.

239610.explanation: There are too few affordable homes in Overbetuwe. If building outside the village boundary is an option, we are in favor.

239612.explanation: People living in poverty must be supported to actively change their situation. Raising the income limit maintains the current situation.

239613.explanation: Performing social work is a stepping stone to paid employment.

239614.explanation: Long-term unemployed people should be actively helped to find paid work, which doesn't have to be through a subsidy.

239615.explanation: People receiving social assistance who are informal caregivers should, under certain conditions, be exempted from the job application requirement.

239616.explanation: We do not have the financial means to pay for this.

239618.explanation: In Overbetuwe, the car is an important means of transportation between the villages. On one hand due to the distances, on the other because there is insufficient public transport.

239619.explanation: Overbetuwe is an attractive municipality for shopping. One of the factors is that parking is free. We want to keep it that way.

239620.explanation: The current public transport is insufficient to travel between our villages within a reasonable time.

239624.explanation: This applies not only to Elst but to all villages.

239625.explanation: A suitable location with sufficient facilities and a population size to accommodate 300 asylum seekers must be found. In Overbetuwe, only the largest village (Elst) meets these criteria.

239626.explanation: But a bike street where cars are guests.

239627.explanation: Not all dikes are suitable for intensive traffic from cars and motorcycles.

239628.explanation: We see no reason to use having children or not as a distinguishing criterion.

239629.explanation: Dental care is important for health. Money should not be a barrier to it.

239630.explanation: The trade-off between expansion and the impact on the health of local residents is not yet clear. Pending that, it is advisable to hold off.

239631.explanation: Mobile home pitches are part of our housing policy and are included in it.

239633.explanation: By realizing a station in Valburg, public transport will receive a new boost.

239634.explanation: Dog tax is paid only by dog owners but has nothing to do with the costs of keeping a dog. The proceeds flow directly into the general funds. We support either abolishing it or spending the proceeds on dog walking areas and other facilities.

239635.explanation: Parking must be free. But in places where a lot of parking space is needed, a limited time duration can be set.

239637.explanation: The Dorpsstraat must become a bike street where cars are guests. However, cars should still have access.

239638.explanation: Wind turbines are an important part of the energy transition. We believe it is important to critically assess which locations cause the least nuisance (noise, shadow flicker). So not blocking them, but weighing carefully.

239639.explanation: In Overbetuwe, the car is an important means of transportation between the villages. On one hand due to the distances, on the other because there is insufficient public transport.

239640.explanation: There is a great demand in Overbetuwe for homes under €250,000, especially for starters. We find it important to build where there is demand.

239641.explanation: Fewer providers leads to less fragmentation, more efficiency, and thus cost reduction.

239642.explanation: If closed off, through traffic (recreational) will pass through Heteren and Driel, increasing traffic pressure in those villages. We do support traffic-calming measures, such as speed bumps.

239589.explanation: The property tax (ozb) should be lowered. This should be considered because property prices are rising. This also keeps us attractive as a place to live.

239590.explanation: A good thing to keep the municipality attractive. A healthy SME sector is only good for the municipality.

239591.explanation: The agricultural sector is a good nature manager. We hold this sector in high regard.

239592.explanation: Stimulating sustainable energy is important. We are also stewards of the earth.

239593.explanation: We want to support the local economy. However, attention must be paid to where businesses are located.

239594.explanation: Status holders must be housed. In this, the interests of the local population should not be overlooked.

239595.explanation: The municipality must carefully consider how money is spent.

239596.explanation: This can be done in consultation with the municipality. Solutions should be sought together with entrepreneurs.
